pattern: The cheapest way to reach any cell from the top is fixed once the cheapest way to reach the three cells above it (straight up or diagonally) is known, so the answer can be filled in one row at a time.
hints: Process the grid row by row, keeping only the best running total for each column reached so far.
A cell's best total is its own value plus the smallest of the totals directly above it and diagonally above-left and above-right, whichever of those exist.
The two edge columns only have two cells above them instead of three, since one diagonal falls off the grid; the final answer is the smallest total across the last row.

edge_inputs.description: a small three by three grid with a clear cheapest path
a two by two grid with negative values
a single cell, the smallest possible grid
a single cell at the minimum value bound
the cheapest path must hug the left edge, exercising the missing left-diagonal neighbor
the cheapest path must hug the right edge, exercising the missing right-diagonal neighbor
values spanning both extremes of the allowed range
every cell is identical, so many falling paths tie for the minimum

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    matrix = [[rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)] for _ in range(n)]
    return [matrix]

stress.description: grid at the maximum constraint size
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
